reason:
Both answers correctly identify alert hygiene as the primary fix, recommend an audit-every-page process with an actionable/urgent bar, suggest routing non-urgent alerts to business hours, set a page-budget target, and correctly conclude that the 1-in-6 rotation is sustainable and shouldn't be restructured (implicitly addressing why follow-the-sun isn't needed). Neither explicitly mentions reducing sprint commitment during on-call weeks, so both miss that key point equally.

The substantive content is essentially equivalent. The main difference is presentation: Answer A prepends a "Sketch:" section that exposes scratch/planning notes before the real answer, which is unnecessary clutter for the reader. Answer B goes straight into a clean, well-structured answer with a numbered action list and ends with a crisp, memorable warning ("you're just redistributing misery"). Answer B is marginally tighter and more polished without sacrificing any content.
